steps.title: Identify the prey quality contrast
steps.content: The passage directly compares two feeding strategies: non-tool-using otters eat “energy-poor” snails, while tool-using otters access “high-quality” mussels and clams. This quality difference is the decisive signal.

steps.title: Match to energy requirements
steps.content:
If both groups of otters have the same energy needs, but snails are energy-poor while mussels and clams are high-quality, non-tool-using otters must process larger quantities of prey to meet those needs.
  • A mischaracterizes tooth damage (the passage shows tool-users avoid it)
  • B contradicts the passage's claim that tool-using otters gain fitness benefits
  • D references an impossible scenario (non-tool-users cannot access hard-shelled prey by biting alone)
.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
